text:
I'll tell a short story about a man who's an ardent birdwatcher. It started one autumn when he walked through his favorite woods on a crisp morning. The dew was still sparkling on the leaves. Birds were singing their cheerful morning songs. He found a bench under an ancient oak, perfect for observing wildlife.

There he sat watching warblers flittering from branch to branch. They were tiny and inconventible. He admired their cheerful behavior.

Several hours later, he noticed something different. A young hawk was circling high above. It landed on an branch a few feet from the man's perch. He felt alert. There couldn't be just any hawk; could it be a peregrine? The hawk's eyes were sharp.

Our hero couldn't figure out if the bird was hunting or just playing. It bobbed its head.

Oh, what fun it must be.

It didn't need hunting rings.

It simply fluttered.

Perhaps it was seeking a mate
